fact: m01: ASR baseline, Th=20 + average re-referencing
m02: nASR, no Neighborhood, non-overlap covariance, learnable weights=True
m03: nASR, Neighborhood=True, non-overlap covariance, learnable weights=True
m04: nASR, Neighborhood=True, full-window covariance, learnable weights=True
m05: nASR, Neighborhood=True, non-overlap covariance, learnable weights=False
Subject a: m05 best test BA=0.677, test Acc=0.680; m02 close second 0.675/0.676
Subject a: m01 highest val BA=0.688 but lower test BA=0.624 (overfitting)
Subject f: m02 best across all metrics, BA=0.618, F1=0.660, Acc=0.623
m01 ranked worst on test for both subjects despite competitive validation
Subject a convergence: m01 epoch 22 (late), m02/m03 epoch 7, m04 epoch 4 (early), all nASR in 5-15 zone
Subject f convergence: m01 epoch 10, nASR models epoch 6-8
m01 inference: ~8.9ms (subject a), ~8.1ms (subject f); nASR variants: 1.0-1.3ms
6-8x inference time reduction for nASR vs traditional ASR
Subject a Pareto: m02 (1.0ms) and m05 (0.680 acc at 1.1ms) on frontier
Subject f Pareto: m02 alone on frontier (fastest + most accurate)
m01 reconstructed >30% noisy windows across all channels; nASR only small subset
4790 noisy windows analyzed in noise mask comparison for Subject a
Full-window covariance (m04) detrimental - overlapping segments redundant and harmful
Learnable weights may add unnecessary complexity (m05 best for Subject a)
Neighborhood underperforms global mean - correlated residual noise in nearby channels
Future work: replace simple averaging with EEGReXferNet-style generative reconstruction
Limitation: only 2 subjects from single dataset; needs broader validation
